paper.title: Search for Distant Hypervelocity Star Candidates Using RR Lyrae Stars

extraction.summary: Extracted 165 HV-RRL candidates (64 gold, 23 silver, 78 bronze) from Fu et al. 2025. All exceed local escape velocity in tangential component with P_unb>0.5.

method_chain.summary: Adopt W22 and L23 RR Lyrae catalogs with metallicity, distance, and radial velocity from LAMOST/SDSS spectroscopy (W22) or Gaia photometric metallicity (L23)
outputs: 138857 RRLs with RA, Dec, metallicity, distance, period

source_refs.context: Sample selection from W22 and L23 catalogs

method_chain.summary: Cross-match with Gaia DR3 to retrieve proper motions
outputs: RRLs with Gaia DR3 proper motions

source_refs.context: Cross-match with Gaia DR3

method_chain.summary: Apply Gaia DR3 astrometric quality cuts: RUWE<1.4, ipd_gof_harmonic_amplitude<0.4, ipd_frac_multi_peak<2, astrometric_excess_noise_sig<=2.0, visibility_periods_used>=13

source_refs.context: Astrometric quality control criteria

method_chain.summary: Distances derived from period-absolute magnitude-metallicity (PMZ) relation for RRLs; typical uncertainty ~10%

source_refs.context: Distance estimation from PMZ relation
Distance uncertainty approximately 10 percent

method_chain.summary: Calculate Galactocentric distance R_GC and tangential velocity v_GC,t using solar position and motion parameters; Monte Carlo with 2000 realizations

source_refs.context: Velocity calculation and Monte Carlo

method_chain.summary: Adopt MWPotential2014 (galpy) consisting of Miyamoto-Nagai disk, power-law bulge, and NFW dark matter halo to compute escape velocity
outputs: v_esc as function of Galactocentric radius

source_refs.context: MWPotential2014 escape velocity calculation

method_chain.summary: Compute unbound probability P_unb from fraction of 2000 Monte Carlo samples where v_GC > v_esc; select P_unb>0.5 and sigma_vGC/vGC<=0.3
outputs: 6838 RRLs with P_unb>0.5 and reliable velocities

source_refs.context: Escape velocity and unbound probability calculation

method_chain.summary: Light curve examination using Gaia DR3, ZTF, and Catalina Sky Survey to verify RRL identification and subtype classification

source_refs.context: Light curve quality examination

method_chain.summary: Classify candidates into gold, silver, and bronze samples based on light curve quality

source_refs.context: Light curve quality classification

method_chain.summary: Paper reports values from input catalogs or supplementary data without reprocessing

inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Doubtful light curve; difficult to confidently identify as RRL or potentially misclassified. Distance and velocity measurements may be unreliable.
